name: Bachelor of Architecture and M.B.A. in Management of Technology

dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: The architecture spine is coherent (the rich sampled pool spans studios depth 0-7 plus Structures, ECS, and a strong technology-elective set: ARCH 335 Digital Tectonics, 337 BIM, 361 Adaptive Paradigms, 423 Advanced Construction, 429 Advanced Structures, 461 Resilient Structural Design). But the displayed plan mixes full architecture years with an unstated graduate M.B.A. block and truncates at 'Fourth Year 2nd Semester' (only 2 items, null credits), so the combined progression is not fully legible.
dimensions.suggestion: Present the complete integrated timeline including the graduate M.B.A.-MOT terms, and show where the technology-management electives (ARCH 335/337) bridge into the business curriculum.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: The architecture prerequisite graph is sound (deepestPrereqChain 6), but this program carries 19 dangling references (missingCoursesSample includes ACCT 615, FIN 516/600/618/624, HRM 601, MGMT 640/645/680/692, ARCH 650/651/652). These are legitimately graduate business/architecture courses outside the undergraduate catalog, so the count is a combined-degree data caveat rather than a true structural defect, but it still hurts on-page navigability.
dimensions.suggestion: Cross-link the graduate M.B.A. and ARCH 6xx courses to the graduate catalog so the 19 references resolve instead of dangling, and resolve the GER 200/300 placeholders.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No program-level learning outcomes appear in the data; only the plan and course descriptions are present. For a design-plus-technology-management dual degree this is a real gap, since the differentiator is precisely the fusion of architectural and technology-management competencies.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish combined learning outcomes covering NAAB design competencies and Management-of-Technology outcomes (innovation, entrepreneurship, tech strategy).

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: The metrics show summedTermCredits 108 against statedTotalCredits 36, a creditReconcileDelta of +72.0. The stated 36 evidently represents only the graduate M.B.A.-MOT overlay, so this large delta is a combined-degree data-consistency caveat rather than a defect, but as presented the page does not reconcile or show the true combined total.
dimensions.suggestion: State the full combined credit total and its split (B.Arch core vs. M.B.A.-MOT overlay) so the 36-credit figure is contextualized and the plan reconciles.

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: Terms are labeled and most rows legible, but the final term is truncated to two courses with null credits, placeholder rows recur ('ARCH Elective iii'), and description excerpts leak contact-hour boilerplate (ARCH 196/296). The graduate M.B.A. portion is not depicted at all.
dimensions.suggestion: Complete the truncated fourth-year term, depict the graduate M.B.A.-MOT terms, and clean the placeholder and boilerplate rows.

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: A B.Arch combined with an M.B.A. in Management of Technology is strongly relevant to design-technology entrepreneurship, firm innovation, and proptech leadership, and the elective pool already leans that way (AD 325 Entrepreneurship for Designers, ARCH 335 Digital Tectonics, 337 BIM, 361 Adaptive Paradigms). The main currency gap is that these technology bridges are elective, and the required computing course (CS 104) is dated.
dimensions.suggestion: Require a BIM/computational course and foreground AD 325 Entrepreneurship and ARCH 335/337 as explicit design-technology bridges into the M.B.A. block.

descriptionQuality.justification: The architecture and technology-elective descriptions are often rich and specific (ARCH 361 Adaptive Paradigms on kinetic/responsive architecture, ARCH 423 Advanced Construction, ARCH 429 Advanced Structures, ARCH 335 Digital Tectonics), but continuation studios (ARCH 196/296/396) are thin/boilerplate, AD 325 Entrepreneurship has an empty description, and no M.B.A. course descriptions are shown.

prerequisiteClarity.justification: Clarity is materially reduced by 19 dangling references to graduate business/architecture courses (ACCT/FIN/HRM/MGMT, ARCH 650-652) that are not in the undergraduate catalog, plus unparenthesized compound architecture prereqs (ARCH 464, ARCH 483 'ARCH 224, ARCH 304, ARCH 314 or (ARCH 323, ARCH 304, ARCH 327)'). The dangling count is largely a combined-degree caveat, but on the page the prerequisites read as unresolved.

missingTopics.topic: Technology entrepreneurship and venture creation for design
missingTopics.rationale: AD 325 Entrepreneurship for Designers exists (and even lacks a description) but is not clearly required; a design-plus-MOT degree should make venture creation and tech-startup practice a core, well-documented component.
The plan lacks a course connecting building technology to the technology-management theme (construction platforms, proptech, digital delivery), the obvious intersection of this dual degree.
ARCH 335 Digital Tectonics and 337 BIM sit in electives while the only required computing is the dated CS 104; a technology-management architect should have required computational fluency.

missingTopics.topic: Innovation strategy and IP for the built environment
missingTopics.rationale: No course addresses managing innovation, intellectual property, or technology strategy specific to design/construction, a core Management-of-Technology competency.

missingTopics.topic: Product and service design / design thinking at scale
missingTopics.rationale: The technology-management pairing would benefit from design-thinking and product-management content linking architectural design methods to broader technology products.

emergingTrends.trend: Generative AI and computational design tooling
emergingTrends.rationale: AI-driven design and estimating are core to technology-managed practice; ARCH 335 Digital Tectonics and the studio sequence should incorporate generative workflows, replacing the aging CS 104 emphasis.

emergingTrends.trend: Proptech, digital twins, and smart buildings
emergingTrends.rationale: ARCH 361 Adaptive Paradigms and BIM (ARCH 337) point toward smart, sensor-driven buildings and digital twins that the MOT curriculum can frame as managed technology platforms.
AD 325 Entrepreneurship plus the technology electives position graduates to launch or lead construction-tech ventures, a fast-growing sector.
ARCH 423 Advanced Construction and digital-fabrication content set up a required maker/robotics component aligned with technology-management practice.
Management-of-Technology skills applied to project and portfolio analytics are increasingly decisive in design-firm competitiveness.

topSuggestions: State the true combined credit total and B.Arch/M.B.A. split so the +72 reconciliation gap (36 stated vs. 108 summed) is contextualized and the plan reads coherently.
Publish combined learning outcomes fusing NAAB design competencies with Management-of-Technology outcomes.
Require a BIM/computational-design course and make AD 325 Entrepreneurship and ARCH 335/337 explicit bridges into the M.B.A.-MOT block; modernize the dated CS 104.
Cross-link the 19 dangling graduate references (ACCT/FIN/HRM/MGMT, ARCH 650-652) to the graduate catalog and resolve GER 200/300.
Complete the truncated fourth-year term and clean thin/boilerplate descriptions (including the empty AD 325 entry).
